summary: A single functional test failed due to network errors from GitHub returning 502 Bad Gateway responses. The test attempted to install a package from GitHub but max retries were exceeded after multiple 502 errors.
root_cause: HTTPSConnectionPool to github.com returned 'too many 502 error responses', causing max retries to be exceeded when attempting to download a test package from GitHub.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicit error message showing GitHub returned multiple 502 errors, indicating server-side issues, not pip code failure
Test is marked as a network test, confirming it requires external network connectivity
Test was retried but failed again with the same network error, typical of transient infrastructure issues
Only one test failure among 1080+ functional tests, with all unit tests passing, indicating isolated infrastructure issue not code defect

suggested_fix: This is a transient network failure from GitHub's infrastructure. No code fix is needed. The test should pass on retry once GitHub's 502 errors resolve.
